title: K6: upgrade recall omitted_candidate_count -> structured dropped[]{id,reason}
description: fab_recall currently signals truncated candidates with a bare `omitted_candidate_count: number` (recallOutputSchema, api-contracts.ts:508; produced in plan-context.ts and consumed in recall.ts service line 210 to build next_steps). Upgrade it to a structured `dropped: { id: string, reason: <enum> }[]` form, reusing fab_archive_scan's existing `dropped[]{session_id, reason: z.enum([...])}` template (api-contracts.ts:572-577) as the shape. All tools converge on ONE controlled reason vocabulary — do NOT let recall invent a private enum. reject / idempotent-skip paths should also emit structured reasons where they drop candidates. Migrate every consumer/doc reference to omitted_candidate_count in the same PR (migrate-before-delete).
action: (1) Schema: in api-contracts.ts add to recallOutputSchema a `dropped: z.array(z.object({ id: z.string(), reason: <SharedDropReasonEnum> }))` and REMOVE `omitted_candidate_count` (line 508). Define a shared reason enum ONCE — extend/reuse the existing archive-scan enum source so both tools draw from one vocabulary; for recall the controlled values are `z.enum(['retrieval_budget','rejected','idempotent_skip'])` (retrieval_budget replaces the old omitted-by-top_k count; rejected/idempotent_skip cover the other drop paths). Co-locate this enum near the archive-scan dropped[] definition (api-contracts.ts:572-577) so future tools import the same union. (2) Producer: in plan-context.ts (line ~540, ~582) and recall service (recall.ts:210, buildNextSteps) replace the `omitted_candidate_count` number with a `dropped[]` array carrying {id, reason:'retrieval_budget'} for each budget-omitted candidate; preserve the next_steps hint text but derive count from dropped.length. (3) MCP description: index.ts:223 references `omitted_candidate_count > 0` — rewrite to reference `dropped[]`. (4) Migrate-before-delete: grep `omitted_candidate_count` across packages/cli, packages/server (incl. plan-context.test.ts:404/720/834/847/883/907/936, recall.test.ts:300-316), and docs (.fabric/AGENTS.md and the MCP server-instructions string that says `omitted_candidate_count > 0`); update every live reference to dropped[]. NOTE: plan-context.ts is the actual producer — recall is a consumer; decide whether plan-context keeps an internal count and recall maps to dropped[], OR plan-context emits dropped[] end-to-end. Prefer plan-context emitting dropped[]{id,reason} end-to-end so the wire shape is consistent.

criteria: RED first: extend recall.test.ts (the existing test at line ~300 'surfaces omitted_candidate_count...') to assert `result.dropped` is an array of `{id, reason}` with reason==='retrieval_budget' and length 1 (replacing `expect(result.omitted_candidate_count).toBe(1)`); it fails before the impl change.
grep-verifiable: `grep -n 'dropped' packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts` shows recallOutputSchema now contains a `dropped` array (in addition to the archive-scan one).

grep-verifiable: `grep -rn 'omitted_candidate_count' packages/shared/src packages/server/src` returns ZERO matches (fully migrated — schema, producer, consumer, tests, index.ts description string all updated).

grep-verifiable: `grep -rn 'omitted_candidate_count' .fabric/AGENTS.md` returns ZERO matches (doc migrated) — and the MCP server-instructions string that read `omitted_candidate_count > 0` now references dropped[].

Reuse check: recall's dropped[] reason is a z.enum drawn from a SINGLE shared vocabulary co-located with archive-scan's enum — `grep -n 'retrieval_budget\|idempotent_skip' packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts` matches and the enum is defined once, not duplicated per tool.
Schema-edit gate: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build` run BEFORE server vitest (api-contracts.ts changed).
`pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server exec vitest run` is green (incl. updated plan-context.test.ts and recall.test.ts).
convergence.verification: cd /Users/wepie/Desktop/personal-projects/pcf && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build && grep -rn 'omitted_candidate_count' packages/shared/src packages/server/src .fabric/AGENTS.md; echo 'expect zero above'; pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server exec vitest run
convergence.definition_of_done: fab_recall reports truncated/dropped candidates as a structured dropped[]{id,reason} list drawn from one shared reason vocabulary (matching fab_archive_scan), the bare omitted_candidate_count is gone everywhere (schema, producer, consumer, tests, MCP description, AGENTS.md), and all gates are green.

files.target: recallOutputSchema line ~505-508 + a shared reason enum near line ~572-577
files.change: Remove omitted_candidate_count; add `dropped: z.array(z.object({id: z.string(), reason: <shared enum>}))`; define the recall reason enum z.enum(['retrieval_budget','rejected','idempotent_skip']) co-located with archive-scan's dropped[] as the single vocabulary source.
Emit dropped[]{id, reason:'retrieval_budget'} end-to-end instead of an omitted count; update the PlanContextResult type at line 161.
Derive next_steps hint from dropped.length (reason==='retrieval_budget') instead of omitted_candidate_count; forward dropped[] onto the recall result.
Assert result.dropped is [{id, reason:'retrieval_budget'}] instead of omitted_candidate_count===1.
Update omitted_candidate_count assertions to the dropped[] shape (toHaveProperty/not.toHaveProperty adjusted to dropped).
Migrate doc text to dropped[]; also update the MCP server-instructions string referencing omitted_candidate_count > 0.

implementation: RED: edit recall.test.ts:300 test to expect result.dropped === [{id: <stable_id>, reason:'retrieval_budget'}] and length 1; confirm it fails.

GREEN schema: in api-contracts.ts remove recallOutputSchema.omitted_candidate_count (line 508); add `dropped: z.array(z.object({id: z.string(), reason: recallDropReason}))`; define `const recallDropReason = z.enum(['retrieval_budget','rejected','idempotent_skip'])` co-located with archive-scan's dropped[] (line ~572) and document it as the shared drop-reason vocabulary.

GREEN producer: in plan-context.ts replace the `omitted_candidate_count` count (lines 161/540/582) with a dropped[]{id, reason:'retrieval_budget'} array end-to-end; carry it through to the recall service.

GREEN consumer: in recall.ts buildNextSteps (line 210) compute `const omitted = result.dropped?.filter(d => d.reason==='retrieval_budget').length ?? 0` and keep the existing next_steps hint wording; ensure dropped[] is on the returned recall result.

Migrate: rewrite index.ts:223 description; update plan-context.test.ts assertions (404/720/834/847/883/907/936); migrate .fabric/AGENTS.md + the MCP server-instructions `omitted_candidate_count > 0` line to dropped[].
Verify zero residual `omitted_candidate_count` in packages/shared/src + packages/server/src + .fabric/AGENTS.md.

success_metrics: RED test failed before impl, green after
recallOutputSchema has dropped[], omitted_candidate_count fully removed
single shared reason vocabulary (not a private enum)
zero residual omitted_candidate_count in src + AGENTS.md
shared dist rebuilt before server tests

reference.pattern: fab_archive_scan dropped[]{session_id, reason: z.enum([...])} (api-contracts.ts:572-577) is the verbatim template

rationale.chosen_approach: Reuse archive-scan's dropped[] shape and a single shared reason enum so all tools converge on one controlled drop-reason vocabulary; emit dropped[] end-to-end from plan-context (the real producer) to keep the wire shape consistent.
rationale.tradeoffs: plan-context.ts is the structural producer (not recall) — the change ripples through plan-context + its tests; depends_on TASK-001 because both edit recall.ts and must not collide.

risks: Missing a consumer of omitted_candidate_count (CLI/skills/docs) -> silent stale reference; the zero-residual grep across packages/shared/src + packages/server/src + .fabric/AGENTS.md is the round-trip oracle.
Bash grep can give false negatives on this repo (ugrep) — use the Grep tool or `node`-based search for the correctness-critical zero-residual check, not raw Bash grep alternation.
Forgetting shared build after schema edit -> invalid_union_discriminator at server test runtime.
plan-context.ts being the actual producer (not recall.ts) means the count lives upstream — do not only patch recall and leave plan-context emitting the old field.
